answer:
A.

- Skeptical senior engs distrust 2nd-person fear-bait; "Hiding in Your Config" pattern-matches to content marketing, triggers "not my config" dismissal.
- A is 1st-person postmortem/case-study framing — HN/Lobsters staple, promises concrete numbers + reproducible method, invites verification not persuasion.
- B presumes reader's mistake w/o evidence, $340k is your number not theirs — reads as false generalization.
- Caveat: A must deliver real baseline #s & method, else "80%" gets flamed as "we were just wasteful." If baseline was embarrassing, consider "How We Cut Our AWS Bill 80% by Fixing One Autoscaling Mistake" — keeps specificity, keeps 1st person.
